text.title: Side-Project Financial Runway Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Side-Project Financial Runway application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Burn = ALL personal + business monthly costs (housing, food, health insurance, hosting, taxes, savings). Do not exclude line items.
rule.rationale: Founders systematically underestimate burn 30-50%; explicit checklist prevents.

rule.statement: Leave-job trigger: ≥12 months runway at current burn AND MRR > 30% of burn AND 3-month MRR growth >0%. Below = stay.
rule.rationale: <12 months runway = panic decisions; 30% MRR floor = the curve isn't speculative.

rule.statement: Model alternative: if relocate to lower-cost geography (Lisbon, Mexico City, Chiang Mai), burn drops 30-60%. Run scenario.
rule.rationale: Geo-arbitrage often unlocks 'leave job' 6-12 months earlier; explicit scenario forces consideration.

rule.statement: Model: what if MRR stays flat for 12 months? What if -20%? Plan B (return to work, scope-down) for both.
rule.rationale: Optimism bias: founders model only growth; stress tests catch fragility before the cliff.

rule.statement: Quarterly: update burn + MRR + buffer. Triggers recompute; do not 'set and forget'.
rule.rationale: Personal finances shift quarterly; quarterly revisit catches drift toward unsustainable.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
